page: Microsoft Azure E64_v4 is an Ev4-family memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this server provides 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with Hyper-Threading enabled. The processor operates at a base frequency of 2.6 GHz and features a 35 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 504.0 GB of RAM, yielding approximately 7.88 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on remote storage options.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-threaded capabilities and memory performance. The server achieves a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 254.85 GB/sec and a Passmark memory score of 2917.59, both placing in the top 25% tier. Database operations on Passmark also show strong performance at 22,694.2. However, single-core performance is average, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1486.18 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1328.0. Large-block memory write bandwidth is measured at 88.57 GB/sec. LLM text generation performance is weak, yielding 6.55 tokens/sec on a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E64_v4 offers high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of GPU hardware. The lack of bundled local storage requires external disk attachment, which should be factored into architectural planning. This server is highly suited for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, enterprise applications, and multi-threaded data processing tasks such as decompression and compilation.
description: Microsoft Azure E64_v4 is an Ev4-family memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 504.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, this x86_64 server utilizes Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization to deliver 32 physical cores with Hyper-Threading. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, making it reliant on network-attached storage. Performance benchmarks highlight strong multi-threaded capabilities, with top-tier results in bzip3 decompression and Passmark database operations. Conversely, single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds remain average to weak. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive enterprise applications, in-memory databases, and heavy multi-threaded data processing workloads that do not require local NVMe drives.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 504 GB of RAM, and strong multi-threaded performance for database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64_v4 is an Intel-powered, memory-optimized VM with 64 vCPUs and 504 GB RAM, designed for databases and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated vCPUs and over five hundred gigabytes of RAM for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 504.0 GB of RAM providing high memory density
Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with x86_64 architecture and 35 MB L3 cache
Strong multi-threaded performance in bzip3 decompression and database operations
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
